steps.title: Identify the breakdown of the honest signal
steps.content:
The passage establishes that saturated feather colors are “honest signals” because carotenoids both produce the color AND confer health—so color reflects fitness.

But tanagers use microstructures to create appearance of saturation without necessarily eating carotenoids.

This breaks the appearance-fitness link.

steps.title: Connect the separation to the signal type
steps.content:
A signal is “honest” only if it truthfully indicates fitness.

Since tanagers can appear saturated without being healthy/fit, their appearance is deceptive—a dishonest signal.

The alternatives either call it honest signaling despite the deception (A), compare effectiveness not discussed in the passage (B), or address a different aspect entirely (C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
